summary: SPIKE: Fuzz test BSON libraries using GenAI
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
To improve our security posture and confidence, we want to fuzz test our BSON libraries across all supported languages. We propose a 2-week spike using [Devin|https://www.cognition.ai/blog/introducing-devin] from Cognition to quickly generate fuzz tests for one BSON library. We will evaluate the effort required and quality of the resulting tests to determine if this is a tenable path forward to increase our security test coverage for our drivers and related libraries.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Users of our drivers and libraries.
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Security vulnerabilities require fixes, CVEs, and upgrades by end users.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
All software can contain security vulnerabilities. The intention with this project is to detect and catch them early preferably before we ship a release.  
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
The severity is highly dependent on the ease of exploit and consequences. Detecting vulnerabilities through fuzz testing will catch errors sooner and mitigate the reputational harm.
h4. Is this issue urgent?
We want to complete the spike in FY25Q4.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
We will compare the ease of specifying requirements to Devin versus a human engineer and the quality of fuzz testing code produced. The desire is to reduce laborious, repetitive coding tasks and allow our human engineers to focus on higher-value work requiring human ingenuity. If this spike proves successful, we would plan to do Devin-implemented fuzz tests across other drivers in a future quarter. We also hope to learn whether further security-focused tests could be generated efficiently using Devin.


{panel}

summary: Do not send buildInfo or buildinfo to check for feature compatibility
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Drivers must not use buildInfo to detect server feature compatibility because buildInfo is not included in the versioned API.
h3. *Motivation*

The [specification README|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/df6be82f865e9b72444488fd62ae1eb5fca18569/README.rst#check-wire-version-not-server-version] notes:
{quote}When determining server capabilities within the driver, rely only on the maxWireVersion in the hello response, not on the X.Y.Z server version.
{quote}
CSHARP-3480 describes a driver using the {{buildInfo}} command as part of the connection handshake. {{buildInfo}} is not part of the [versioned API|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/versioned-api/#api-v1-commands] and will return an error if sent with apiStrict. Example:
{code}$ mongosh --apiVersion=1 --apiStrict --eval "db.runCommand({buildInfo:1})"
Current Mongosh Log ID: 61194b0fea88cad1d1192c7a
Connecting to:          mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/?directConnection=true&serverSelectionTimeoutMS=2000
Using MongoDB:          (API Version 1)
Using Mongosh:          1.0.4

For mongosh info see: https://docs.mongodb.com/mongodb-shell/

MongoServerError: Provided apiStrict:true, but the command buildInfo is not in API Version 1. Information on supported commands and migrations in API Version 1 can be found at https://dochub.mongodb.org/core/manual-versioned-api
{code}
The server may remove full support for {{buildInfo}} in the future.
h4. Who is the affected end user?

All driver users.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

If buildinfo or buildInfo is removed from the server in the future, this could prevent a driver that is sending buildinfo or buildInfo from connecting to a server.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

buildinfo / buildInfo is not included in the versioned API. This will prevent the server
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

A problem would be high severity. If buildInfo is removed from the server, drivers that are sending buildInfo as part of connection establishment will fail to connect.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

TBD. This will be a requirement when the server removes buildInfo.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

It may be necessary to coordinate with the server team. A driver that has not resolved this will not be able to connect to an upcoming server release that removes buildInfo.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

No. There is functional impact.
h3. *Scope*

Add a note to the Handshake specification to prohibit any other commands being sent to determine feature compatibility.

Add a new specification test in the unified test format to test this behavior.
- Enable a failpoint on the buildInfo and buildinfo commands to return a network error.
- Send a command (could be ping).
- Expect success. Connection establishment should fail on drivers sending buildInfo/buildinfo.
{panel}

summary: OIDC teardown script fails with "ERROR: CLUSTER_NAME not set"
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

The teardown phase of the [OIDC auth tests|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc] fails with this error:

{noformat}
ERROR: CLUSTER_NAME not set
{noformat}

I believe it's coming from [teardown-atlas-cluster.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/atlas/teardown-atlas-cluster.sh#L30], which is called by [auth_oidc/teardown.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/teardown.sh#L16]. The root cause seems to be that {{CLUSTER_NAME}} is not set. The [Azure|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc/azure] and [GCP|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc/gcp] OIDC tests don't have the same problem, but they write {{CLUSTER_NAME}} to file in a different path (see [auth_oidc/gcp/setup.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/gcp/setup.sh#L22] vs [auth_oidc/setup.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/setup.sh#L41]).

The result is that the teardown phase fails consistently, possibly leaving Atlas resources running.

Confirmed this impacts at least the [Rust Driver|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_rust_driver_oidc_linux_oidc_auth_test_latest_7429896ea2f15e1046b9aabb73c4f00f83af20f3_25_03_20_20_19_42/0?type=T#L1505] and [Go Driver|https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_testoidc_variant_oidc_auth_test_a4c0170403e5f6a01ee191dc1e84b3becc7012d2_25_03_21_19_50_47/logs?execution=0]

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Drivers devs, possibly DBX leadership (if we're spending money on unterminated Atlas resources).

h4. How does this affect the end user?

OIDC Evergreen tasks may fail. Maybe wastes money.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

It will always occur when using the standard OIDC test setup and teardown scripts.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Confusion about the failed OIDC Evergreen task, possibly blocked driver development and/or releases. Possible wasted money.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Update OIDC test teardown scripts to correctly tear down resources.

{panel}

summary: $dateToString aggregation with timezone outputs invalid ISO8601 string
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    The current default behavior of MongoDB's $dateToString aggregation expression is incorrect because it violates ISO 8601. (It tacks on a "Z" to the end of the string by default, which implies that it is in UTC [1], but in actually it's in the timezone of the local server.)

This ticket proposes a number of routes to change the default behavior of $dateToString to bring it into compliance with ISO 8601.

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Coordinated_Universal_Time_(UTC)
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    When using the {{$dateToString}} aggregation operator on an ISODate value, specifying a timezone but not a format string, an invalid/inaccurate ISO8601 date string is returned. 

Specifically: {{2023-04-26T12:06:17.194Z}} when using something like:

{code:JavaScript}
db.collection.aggregate([{ "$addFields": {"txDateString": { "$dateToString": {date: "$transactionDateTS", timezone: "America/New_York"} } }} ])`
{code}

The root issue is that the "Z" *categorically implies* a timezone of UTC (+00:00)... But, in this case, that's absolutely incorrect and, technically, makes it an invalid ISO8601 date string. (I suppose one could argue that it's *syntatically* valid - but it's certainly misleading and could result in major issues if another program were to parse the date string as it would interpret the time as "Zulu"/UTC because of the "Z".) The inclusion of the "Z" when *explicitly specifying* a timezone is certainly unexpected...  

At a *minimum*, when a format string is unspecified and a date/time in UTC is converted to another time zone, the "Z" should be left off. Ideally, the UTC offset (obtained from the timezone conversion) should be included as per ISO8601 -- i.e. in this case, {{2023-04-26T12:06:17.194-04:00}} (at the time of this writing, {{America/New_York}} is on Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) which is UTC-04:00.) 

Related issues appear to include: SERVER-33173 and SERVER-28610

summary: Add spec test for server deprioritization with changed ServerDescription
description:
h3. *Summary*
The server selection spec states that deprioritization should use "only server addresses and not ServerDescription objects" to ensure a deprioritized server cannot become eligible for retry due to topology changes. However, the existing spec tests all use identical ServerDescriptions between topology_description.servers and deprioritized_servers, so drivers using full ServerDescription equality instead of address-only comparison will incorrectly pass all tests. 

h3. *Motivation*

Avoid introducing buggy code that attempts to filter candidates based on a server object, e.g. {{\{27017, RSPrimary, 50ms\} != \{27017, RSSecondary, 5ms\}}} would incorrectly pass the filter, allowing the same server (with changed state) to be selected for retry.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Add a unified spec test to cover the following case: 

# Client sends operation to b:27017 (RSPrimary, RTT 50ms)
# Operation fails 
# Driver adds b:27017 to deprioritized list with its current state
# Topology monitor updates and b:27017 stepped down (RSSecondary, RTT 5ms)
# Ensure that b:2717 does not pass the filter and does not get selected for retry

summary: Auth-ssl configuration does not enforce auth on standalone with mongodb-runner
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
When starting a standalone with auth and ssl using {{run-mongodb.sh}} authentication is not enforced. This may cause failures for tests expecting authentication error.

This bug was hidden because {{run-orchestration.sh}} uses the mongo-orchestration HTTP API, which internally added --auth to mongod whenever login/password were present in the config, regardless of auth_key. run-mongodb.sh bypasses mongo-orchestration and passes the config directly to mongodb-runner, which does not have this implicit behavior and relies solely on what is in the config file. 

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers developers
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Not affected
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
If a driver has tests that expect authentication enforced on a standalone
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Test failures
h4. Is this issue urgent?
No
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Bug is fixed and the fix is tested.


{panel}
